authority_note: Starter bounded entries only. Not endorsed for final flight use. All values require operator review and sourcing before operational use.

entries.label: Helium-Xenon Mixture Basis (20/80 mol%)
entries.source_note: Representative He-Xe mixture properties at ~20/80 mol% basis per Brayton cycle literature. Cp/density at ~1.4 MPa operating point. Verify for specific mixture ratio and pressure.
entries.pressure_notes: Pressure-dependent properties. Values stated at ~1.4 MPa reference. Operating pressure range 0.5–3.0 MPa typical.
entries.compatibility_notes: Noble gas — generally compatible with SS, Inconel. Xenon procurement and containment mass penalty significant.
entries.hazards: Asphyxiant at high concentration. Xenon high pressure vessel hazard.

entries.label: Ammonia (NH3) Two-Phase
entries.source_note: ISS ATCS heritage fluid. NIST REFPROP / ASHRAE data. Liquid phase properties at 293 K reference.
entries.pressure_notes: Saturation pressure ~1.0 MPa at 298 K. Design for full two-phase regime.
entries.compatibility_notes: Incompatible with copper, zinc, brass. Compatible with aluminum, steel, titanium with correct passivation.
entries.hazards: Toxic, corrosive, flammable at 15–28% by volume. Personnel exposure limits enforced.

entries.label: Carbon Dioxide (CO2) Supercritical/Two-Phase
entries.source_note: NIST REFPROP data. Supercritical region cp/density at ~8 MPa, 330 K. sCO2 Brayton cycle candidate.
entries.pressure_notes: Critical point 304.1 K / 7.38 MPa. Supercritical operation requires high-pressure design.
entries.compatibility_notes: Compatible with SS, titanium, nickel alloys. Watch moisture contamination.

entries.label: Water (H2O) Single Phase / Two Phase
entries.source_note: NIST IAPWS-IF97. Properties at 293 K / 0.1 MPa. Heritage use in ISS cooling loops.
entries.pressure_notes: Low-pressure operation below 373 K. Freezing hazard below 273 K. Microgravity two-phase management required.
entries.compatibility_notes: Use deionized water. Compatible with SS, titanium, coatings. Risk of biological growth in long-duration loops.
entries.hazards: Freezing hazard in thermal cycling. Electrical hazard on short.

entries.label: Sodium (Na) Liquid Metal
entries.source_note: IAEA liquid metal properties. Values at 800 K operating point. Nuclear plant heritage. Space reactor cooling candidate.
entries.pressure_notes: Near-atmospheric operating pressure. Freeze protection required above 371 K (melt point).
entries.compatibility_notes: Severe fire/explosion risk with water and air. SS-316 compatible at operational temperatures. Nitrogen inert blanket required.
entries.hazards: Pyrophoric. Reacts violently with water. Highly toxic fire products.

entries.label: NaK-78 Eutectic Liquid Metal
entries.source_note: NaK-78 (78% K, 22% Na by mass) eutectic. Liquidus ~262 K. Properties at 600 K from literature. Space reactor application candidate.
entries.pressure_notes: Near-atmospheric operation. Lower melt point than sodium is key advantage.
entries.compatibility_notes: Pyrophoric — same sodium family hazards. Compatible with SS at operation temps. Inert gas cover required.
entries.hazards: Pyrophoric with air and water. Potassium component explosive with water. Personnel hazard significant.

entries.label: Lithium (Li) Liquid Metal
entries.source_note: Properties at 800 K from IAEA / literature. Highest-temp liquid metal loop candidate. Extremely high Cp favors mass efficiency.
entries.pressure_notes: Melt point 454 K requires high freeze-protection margin. Near-atmospheric operation.
entries.compatibility_notes: Reacts with nitrogen. Compatible with refractory metals (Mo, Ta, W, Nb) and some SS grades at temperatures. Strong material selection constraint.
entries.hazards: Pyrophoric. Reacts violently with water and air. High melt point freeze protection critical.
